comment.body:
I'm writing the morning post currently.
Hope, you start to see how I read the markets. What I mean by creative process... and one really has to dig into it
When I say, I don't like shortcuts, nor plp who look for shortcuts, or laziness, this is what I mean.
To write a post it takes me more than 5 hours in avg. A lot of data to check, I sit back, I think about it, recheck the calculations, etc. to get a bias.
And also like that, I know what could disprove me, as I said about the mean at the end of the post.

Today I have classes, and I have to supervise things, so I will be occuped until 2am morning tomorrow, so I won't be here.

comment.body: She said last Thursday that traders were short fly. A short fly spread is a bet on increased IV and bigger swings outside the wings of the fly. And she pointed out that the fly was positioned just above the net zero speed level, and from that + the position size, she figured the trade was probably intentional.. but it actually did the opposite of what was expected. ..Cuz dealers went long the fly, and due to the local speed profile, the opposite effect played out. Customer flow was long vol smirk, but it indirectly caused vol smirk to drop instead.
comment.raw_body: She said last Thursday that traders were short fly. A short fly spread is a bet on increased IV and bigger swings outside the wings of the fly. Customer flow was long vol smirk, but it indirectly caused vol smirk to drop instead.
